claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment. The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know if they are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **Between 16 and 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one - just let me know their ages and we'll assess eligibility for each child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and want to know if I'm eligible.

I have two children - Alex who is 10 years old, and Blake who is 2 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! Thanks for reaching out. I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex (10 years old) and Blake (2 years old).

Great news to start with - since both of your children are under 16 years old, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex and Blake are both under 16, they won't be working full-time, so this check is satisfied. Let me move to the next question.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means checking if either child is receiving benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name. 

Given their ages (10 and 2), this is very unlikely, but I just need to confirm - are both children free from receiving these types of benefits in their own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receives any benefits in their own name. They're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! With a 10-year-old and a 2-year-old, that's exactly what I'd expect. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Given that Alex is 10 and Blake is 2, neither of them would be in an apprenticeship, so we can move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself (and not claiming certain means-tested benefits)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

The most common situation is simply that you normally live in the UK. Is that the case for you, or does one of the other situations apply?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, let me ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people who work for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Members of the Armed Forces stationed abroad
- Diplomatic staff
- Civil servants posted overseas

Or are you living in certain other qualifying countries with special arrangements?

If none of these apply and you're simply living abroad without a UK government role, then unfortunately you may not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit. Could you tell me a bit more about your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I have to deliver some disappointing news.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake at this time.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 10 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 2 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation

Both of your children meet all the child-related requirements for Child Benefit:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working full-time
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship

However, **UK Child Benefit requires the claimant (you) to meet residency requirements**. Since you are living abroad and are not:
- A Crown servant working overseas
- Someone with settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Living in certain qualifying countries with special arrangements

...you do not currently qualify to claim Child Benefit.

### What You Can Do

- **If your circumstances change** and you return to live in the UK, you would likely become eligible to claim Child Benefit for both children
- **If you're working abroad temporarily** for a UK employer, or your situation changes in another way, it may be worth checking again
- For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I understand this isn't the news you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with or clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex (age 10) meets the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working full-time, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant or in a qualifying situation. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake (age 2) meets the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not working full-time, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant or in a qualifying situation. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While both children (Alex, age 10, and Blake, age 2) meet all the child-related eligibility requirements for Child Benefit, the claimant does not satisfy the UK residency requirements. The claimant is currently living abroad and does not fall into any of the qualifying categories (not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in a qualifying country). UK residency is a fundamental requirement for claiming Child Benefit, so unfortunately the family is not eligible at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
